{"key_event":"Liberal-Conservative Fusion and Pérez compromise","reasoning":"Organized parties, newspapers, congressional opposition, the Radical current, and religious toleration moved Chilean civil society from weak toward moderate, though participation remained elite-centered and Mapuche autonomy was being reduced. State capacity stayed strong: executive dominance softened and the war with Spain exposed limits, but fiscal growth, infrastructure, victory in 1859, and Araucanía settlement maintained or slightly increased administrative and coercive reach.","society_change":0.7,"society_power":5.3,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":7.8}
